stories.headline: Amazon is shutting down new sign-ups for Mechanical Turk
stories.blurb: Amazon will no longer accept new customers for Mechanical Turk, the platform that powered early AI training by paying humans to label data. Existing customers can still use it, but the move signals the end of an era for a service that helped bootstrap machine learning before synthetic data and better models made human labeling less critical.
stories.why_it_matters: The platform that trained a generation of AI models is being quietly retired.
stories.original_headline: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk

stories.headline: Anthropic just signed a $19 billion lease to build its own AI campus
stories.blurb: Claude's maker is partnering with TeraWulf to build a dedicated AI infrastructure campus in Kentucky. The 20-year lease could generate $19 billion in revenue for the mining-turned-AI-hosting company, marking one of the largest private AI infrastructure deals on record.
stories.why_it_matters: The biggest AI labs are now signing decade-long leases for their own data centers like Fortune 500s lease office towers.
stories.original_headline: TeraWulf Signs $19 Billion Lease With Anthropic for AI-Infrastructure Campus

stories.headline: Chinese AI video startup Kling just raised $2.8 billion
stories.blurb: Kuaishou Technology is spinning off Kling, its AI video generator, which secured $2.8 billion from investors. The company plans to list the unit separately as competition heats up in text-to-video models.
stories.why_it_matters: One of China's top AI video tools is becoming its own company with serious backing.
stories.original_headline: Kling Raises $2.8 Billion Amid Planned Spinoff From Kuaishou

stories.headline: Alibaba bans employees from using Claude Code
stories.blurb: Alibaba has classified Claude Code as high-risk software and barred employees from using it, according to TechCrunch. The ban comes as companies grapple with security concerns around AI coding assistants that can access and modify codebases.
stories.why_it_matters: Big tech firms are drawing hard lines on which AI tools employees can use with proprietary code.
stories.original_headline: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code

try_this_prompt.title: The hiring red flag translator
try_this_prompt.prompt: I just interviewed a candidate and something feels off, but I can't articulate it. I'll describe what they said and how they said it. Don't tell me to trust my gut. Instead: name the specific behavior I noticed, explain what it predicts about their first 90 days, and give me one question to ask in the next round that will confirm or disprove it.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When your instinct is screaming but your rubric says hire.

daily_question: If you had to explain to a non-technical friend why a company would ban its own employees from using an AI coding tool, what would you say?
